note: Confidence is claim-specific; no package-wide score exists or should be inferred.

claims.claim: Total genesis issuance never exceeds the fixed pool; issued + unissued remainder == pool (floor-sum inequality).
claims.source_locator: DESIGN_001 TECHNICAL_DESIGN.md §6; protocol/allocation.py
claims.evidence_supporting: Standard floor-sum inequality; assert_supply_invariant recomputes every floor; audit repro R1 (2000 exact-integer randomized trials, 0 violations); tamper probes rejected.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: None found.
claims.assumptions_required: Exact integer arithmetic; the deployed implementation matches this reference (only the synthetic reference exists).
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested: the validator recomputes floors from inputs and the audit's independent code re-derives them; tampered records are rejected.
claims.holds_under: All integer inputs within validated ranges.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Only under a divergent reimplementation (the stated Breaker-seat risk); no countermodel exists in-scope.

claims.claim: The synthetic claim verifier is fail-closed: all 34 executable invalid vectors reject with catalogued codes and valid vectors admit.
claims.source_locator: DESIGN_001 verify_evidence.py; fixtures/EXPECTED.json
claims.evidence_supporting: Evidence gate re-run at subject: PASS, 34 executable invalid cases, byte-stable fixture regeneration; 37 unit tests pass.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Vectors and verifier share an author; coverage is bounded by the authored fixture set; crypto is explicitly synthetic stand-ins (HMAC), not Bitcoin or PQ.
claims.assumptions_required: The synthetic model's semantics are the intended spec; fixture catalogue approximates the attack surface.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Partially self-referential: fixtures were authored by the design seat; the 43-vector second-implementation differential (C-03) mitigates but both consume the same fixture corpus.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Real Bitcoin script/sighash/SegWit/Taproot semantics, real PQ signatures, real reorg behavior — all explicitly out of scope (Tracks B/C).

claims.claim: An independent second implementation (JS) agrees with the Python reference on all committed fixtures: 43 agreements, 0 disagreements, 0 crashes.
claims.source_locator: issue #33; experiments/BENEFICIAL_GENESIS_RETEST_002; DIFF_RETEST_002 audit
claims.evidence_supporting: Re-executed by this audit: node verify.mjs → {agreements:43, disagreements:0, crashes:0}.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Agreement is over the shared authored fixture corpus, not over adversarially generated inputs; shared blind spots possible.
claims.assumptions_required: Fixture corpus is representative.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested (cross-implementation), but corpus-bounded.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Inputs outside the corpus; differing raw-parser behaviors not exercised by the corpus.

claims.claim: Linear pro-rata allocation is split-mass invariant up to integer-floor residue: address-splitting yields no material gain.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM FAILURE_CONDITIONS.md preserved finding 1; scenario 06; Breaker DIFFERENTIAL_REVIEW §2
claims.evidence_supporting: floor(P*Σwᵢ/T) vs Σfloor(P*wᵢ/T) differ only by floor residue; audit repro reproduced scenario 06 exactly (gain = -53/25,000,000 for a 400-way split) from fresh code.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: None.
claims.assumptions_required: Linear weighting; splitting preserves total contributed mass.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested; audit re-derived independently.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Does not hold for any strictly concave or capped weighting (that is C-05).

claims.claim: Concave (sqrt/log) and per-identity-capped allocation rules are severely Sybil-exploitable absent an identity layer (e.g. +40.6pp for sqrt/400-way; +70.5pp for 10%-cap/20-way), so no tested allocation formula both resists whales and resists splitting in a permissionless setting.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM MECHANISM_NECESSITY.md §4, FC6; scenarios 05/26; Breaker agreement matrix
claims.evidence_supporting: Concavity implies Σf(w/k)·k > f(w); audit repro reproduced both scenario numbers exactly with fresh code; Grok derived the same direction on different populations.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Magnitudes are population-specific; the deduction gives direction and mechanism, scenarios give magnitude only for their populations.
claims.assumptions_required: Identity creation is free and unlinkable (true of Bitcoin addresses); attacker splits optimally.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested; also proven by pure-math helpers on both sides.
claims.holds_under: Any strictly concave weighting or binding per-identity cap without identity verification.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: An effective identity/Sybil-resistance layer (which the design explicitly does not have and which would contradict its permissionless framing).

claims.claim: Linear pro-rata preserves contribution concentration one-for-one: an honest whale contributing X% of eligible sats receives ~X% of issued units, up to floors.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM scenarios 01-03; preserved finding 1
claims.evidence_supporting: Immediate from the allocation identity; scenario 03 (99% whale → 99.000% share) consistent; audit's exact reproductions confirm the identity.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: None on the math. On interpretation: whether X% concentration is 'unacceptable' is a policy judgment.
claims.assumptions_required: None beyond linearity.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: n/a.

claims.claim: The fixed pool issues in full regardless of subscription level: a single 1,000-sat donor alone in an epoch receives the entire pool; the denominator is realized total, not a target.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM MECHANISM_NECESSITY.md §2; scenarios 19/20; Breaker 19 (top1_issued=1.0)
claims.evidence_supporting: floor(P·T/T)=P for any T>0; audit repro R3 confirms full issuance at T=1 and T=10^15, zero at T=0, and dust-donor floor-to-zero.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: This is a property of the specified rule, not an inevitability — a minimum-subscription floor or flow-scaled pool would change it; neither is specified.
claims.assumptions_required: The design pack's stated allocation rule.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Any redesign adding an issuance floor/scaling — flagged as open redesign space, not modelled.

claims.claim: Conditional rebate arithmetic: given a colluding arrangement at rate r with zero friction, charity retention falls by exactly r·donation and donor net cost falls to (1-r)·donation, for all r in [0,1].
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM FC2 part 1; model/collusion.py; Breaker rebate.py
claims.evidence_supporting: An arithmetic identity; audit repro verified all integer rates 0-100%.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: It is nearly tautological — its epistemic content is the *framing* (the protocol cannot see or prevent it), which is supported by the design's own residual-risk list.
claims.assumptions_required: An arrangement exists (the entire question of incidence is assumed away at this level).
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Tests encode the identity (unavoidable for an identity); the meaningful test is the friction decomposition (C-09).
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: n/a.

claims.claim: Expected (friction-adjusted) rebate incidence collapses far below the conditional figure when access to a colluding genesis charity is rare/costly (e.g. 100,000 vs 2,500,000 sats at 5% access × 80% enforcement).
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM scenario 27; FC2 part 2; Breaker scenario 28
claims.evidence_supporting: Audit repro reproduced the exact figures; the qualitative point (expected ≪ conditional under low access) is robust across any access probability ≪ 1.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: The 5%/80%/30% parameters are invented; nothing anchors real access probability to a genesis charity set that does not exist yet; FATF literature confirms charity-abuse is a real risk class but supplies no rate.
claims.assumptions_required: Multiplicative independence of access/enforcement/detection; parameter ranges span reality.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested as arithmetic; parameters are assumptions, correctly labelled as such post-repair.
claims.holds_under: Any parameterization with small access probability.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: If the genesis set were large, permissively curated, or contained even one attacker-friendly charity at scale, effective access → 1 for informed donors and the conditional figure re-emerges. Charity-set curation is the real control variable and is unmodelled (Track D).

claims.claim: ORIGINAL (retracted): 'rational profit-seeking donor behavior is predicted — not merely possible — to erode aggregate charity benefit.'
claims.source_locator: pre-repair FAILURE_CONDITIONS.md at b588779 (git show)
claims.evidence_supporting: n/a — the claim was withdrawn.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: No behavioral, access, or enforcement model backed the prediction; controlling review REV-002 and Breaker concurred; repair retracted it.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Pre-repair tests asserted the assumption (verified: the zero-cost test asserted '0' as expected value).

claims.claim: The stolen/tainted-fund migration pathway exists: the verifier proves cryptographic control, not legal ownership, so stolen-key donations admit valid claims.
claims.source_locator: DESIGN_001 THREAT_MODEL residual 2; fixture claim_stolen_source_key_crypto_ok; ECON FC4 pathway component
claims.evidence_supporting: The design pack's own fixture demonstrates a crypto-valid stolen-key claim; no ownership oracle exists or is proposed.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: None. Magnitude of resulting real-world flow is a separate empirical question (C-12).
claims.assumptions_required: None.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely demonstrated by an executable fixture.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Only by adding out-of-protocol screening (policy layer).

claims.claim: Net profitability of migrating stolen funds is assumption-conditional: the sensitivity grid contains both profitable and unprofitable cells depending on token value and forgone alternative-realization value; the original 'zero-cost fully profitable laundering' claim was wrong.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM model/tainted_funds.py; scenario 10 grid; REV-001; Breaker laundering.py
claims.evidence_supporting: Audit repro reproduced the default cell and the full grid exactly and confirmed sign flips; external anchor: documented laundering discounts (e.g. Coincheck hackers offering stolen NEM at 15% below market) support alternative_realization < 1 and haircut > 0 as directionally real.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: All probability/haircut parameters are illustrative; the grid's 'token_value_per_unit' column silently switches semantics from the headline cell (audit finding AUD-SEM-01: multiplier used as absolute unit value in the grid vs anchor×multiplier in the headline).
claims.assumptions_required: Attacker rationality; the decomposition's independence structure; parameter ranges bracket reality.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Post-repair tests test decomposition and boundaries; the audit independently re-derived every cell.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Outside the grid (e.g. token value ≫ 1.5× or alternative realization ≈ 0, where migration becomes clearly profitable — i.e. the *more successful the new ledger, the stronger the laundering incentive*; stated nowhere this crisply in the record).

claims.claim: Governance capture by donation size alone occurs if and only if a proportional/token-weighted integration rule is adopted; the allocation mechanism itself fixes no governance rule (52.49% majority for the scenario-13 whale under token_weighted; 1/501 under equal; 0 under none).
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM FC3; model/governance.py; scenario 13; REV-003
claims.evidence_supporting: Audit repro reproduced all scenario-13 numbers exactly (whale share 13121911/24999936 > 1/2).
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: The design pack indeed specifies no governance mapping — but by the same token nothing prevents a future integration from defaulting to token-weighted; conditionality cuts both ways.
claims.assumptions_required: The eventual ledger honors whatever rule is declared (Track E enforcement is open).
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested.
claims.holds_under: Each named rule, at genesis snapshot time.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Durability after transfer/lockup-expiry is unmodelled; a 'none' rule can be undone by later governance decisions of the ledger.

claims.claim: cap_then_renormalize reduced the tested whale's final governance share (~9.77% at a 500bps nominal clip) but is not a hard per-holder cap: renormalization can push final shares above the nominal clip (degenerate: a lone holder ends at 100% under any clip).
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM model/governance.py; scenario 14; micro-repair; RETEST_003 V-002
claims.evidence_supporting: Audit repro matched the committed whale weight exactly (99999749/1023769849 ≈ 9.768%) and independently constructed 1- and 2-holder counterexamples (100% and 50%/50% under a 5% clip).
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: None on the math; on usefulness, the reduction is scenario-specific and the rule's durability past genesis is open Track E work — correctly disclosed post-repair.
claims.assumptions_required: Snapshot-time computation.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested at 1/2/3 holders post-repair.
claims.holds_under: Many-holder populations where unclipped mass is large.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Few-holder or heavily-clipped populations where renormalization dominates (disclosed).

claims.claim: Transferability is not necessary for the charity-receipt and migration-claim-binding functions the design pack specifies; its necessity for payment/staking/fee/bootstrap functions of an unspecified future ledger is open.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM MECHANISM_NECESSITY.md (repaired); REV-004; Breaker concurrence
claims.evidence_supporting: The receipt/claim functions demonstrably complete without transfer (a claim right suffices); the target-function matrix makes the open remainder explicit.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: 'Necessity' is not a theorem over unspecified requirements; the conclusion is a scoping statement, not an empirical result.
claims.assumptions_required: The design pack's stated v1 scope is the correct baseline.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Not a testable claim; correctly reclassified as open after repair.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Any program decision that adds ledger functions requiring a transferable unit.

claims.claim: FC7 ('social benefit is dominated by a simpler non-token mechanism') — partially triggered for specified functions per the packages.
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM FC7; ALTERNATIVES_COMPARISON.md; scenario 21
claims.evidence_supporting: For fixed donation volume, a non-transferable receipt preserves the specified functions while removing the monetization surfaces — that comparison is fair as far as it goes.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: AUDIT FINDING (AUD-MODEL-03): every alternative comparison holds donation volume constant across mechanisms, assuming away participation elasticity — but inducing donations is the mechanism's entire purpose. A transferable reward could raise donation volume (favoring the design) or attract only mercenary/tainted flow (opposing it). No package models this, no reviewer flagged it, and no synthetic model can resolve it. The dominance question is therefore empirically open in both directions.
claims.assumptions_required: Donation volume invariant across compared mechanisms (implicit, unstated, load-bearing).
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Simulations correctly compute what they model; the missing variable is outside all of them.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Any nonzero participation elasticity in either direction.

claims.claim: Denominator/timing exposure: late actors with denominator visibility hold an informational advantage; a late surge dilutes early donors (4.802% → 0.828% in scenario 09); marginal allocation rate itself is timing-neutral under pro-rata; sealed precommitment is a candidate mitigation (Track B feasibility open).
claims.source_locator: ECON_REDTEAM FC5; scenario 09
claims.evidence_supporting: Dilution arithmetic is exact; the information asymmetry follows from the fixed-pool structure; Counterparty's 1-month burn (2014) is a real precedent of participants committing under denominator uncertainty.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Magnitude is scenario-specific; whether ex-ante uncertainty is 'unacceptable' is policy.
claims.assumptions_required: Participants care about final share, not just marginal rate.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely tested for the dilution arithmetic.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Sealed/precommitted designs (feasibility on real Bitcoin untested).

claims.claim: Final disposition: UNDERLYING_MECHANISM: CONTINUE_WITH_CONDITIONS; ECONOMIC_GATE_PASS: false.
claims.source_locator: PR #35 body; FAILURE_CONDITIONS.md aggregate; Breaker DECISION.json; RETEST_003 DECISION
claims.evidence_supporting: Consistent with the evidence in the narrow sense: nothing proves the concept incoherent, and the gate correctly did not pass.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: PROCESS FINDING (AUD-PROC-04): the controlling repair contract *prescribed* this exact disposition before the repair and retest were performed ('Required final disposition: ... CONTINUE_WITH_CONDITIONS ... ECONOMIC_GATE_PASS: false'), and the retest 'preserved (not escalated)' it. The original analysis had concluded REJECT_OR_REDESIGN; its stated reasons were genuinely overclaimed (C-10, C-12), but the replacement verdict was directed by the operator-account reviewer, not independently re-derived. A defensible alternative reading of the same post-repair evidence is 'UNDERDETERMINED pending ledger-function specification and Track D design' — which is close to what CONTINUE_WITH_CONDITIONS means operationally, but the record should not be cited as three seats independently converging on it.
claims.assumptions_required: That the seven conditions attached to continuation are actually enforced by later gates.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Not testable; it is a decision.
claims.confidence: MODERATE (as a reasonable reading) / LOW (as an independent multi-seat conclusion)

claims.claim: The Grok Breaker seat independently confirmed REV-001…006 and re-derived the six narrower findings.
claims.source_locator: PR #37; DIFFERENTIAL_REVIEW.md; DECISION.json
claims.evidence_supporting: File-content clean room verified (all 72 freeze hashes correct; audit-verified); the code-level defect confirmations (lottery replacement, missing renorm, cost=0) are objectively checkable and this audit re-verified each against b588779; the six findings were re-computed on different populations.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: The Breaker read REV-001…007 and the controlling gate disposition before freezing (its own disclosure + issue #36's instruction), so confirmation was directed, not blind; the pre-differential mechanism verdict matched the already-read controlling review's direction; provider identity is self-declared (C-20 context).
claims.assumptions_required: That reading a detailed defect list does not predetermine 'confirmation' of code-checkable facts (defensible for objective code facts; not for judgment calls like REV-004's scope ruling).
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Objective items: genuinely re-verified. Judgment items: anchored.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Judgment-dependent REV items and the mechanism verdict.

claims.claim: The review chain constitutes different-family (cross-provider) independent review.
claims.source_locator: issue #33 lineage; PR #37/#39 language; retest receipt provider_model_label
claims.evidence_supporting: Internally consistent narrative; distinct house styles between packages (Fraction vs float, different APIs, different scenario grids) are weak stylistic evidence of distinct authorship.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Single GitHub account performed every action; no signed commits, session transcripts, or third-party attestation; model labels are self-declared strings; AGENTS.md itself warns fluent repetition cannot promote a draft into evidence. Cross-model agreement, even if the labels are true, is agreement between models given the same prompts, same task framing, and the same controlling review — correlated inputs.
claims.assumptions_required: Operator honesty about which tool produced which artifact.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Not testable from repository bytes.

claims.claim: Receipts and commit bindings are accurate and sufficient to reproduce the record.
claims.source_locator: all three econ RECEIPT.json files; DESIGN receipt
claims.evidence_supporting: Every named commit verified in the graph with claimed contents; binding commits touch only receipts; simulators re-run byte-identically; commands reproduce (185/185 lab tests after npm ci; 43/0/0 differential; PASS doctor).
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Receipts omit the npm-ci prerequisite; 'nexus doctor PASS with WARN WORKTREE_DIRTY before commit' shows honest recording.
claims.assumptions_required: A future reader has the same Python/Node major versions.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Genuinely verified by re-execution.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: CONCAVE_LOG float scheme is disclosed as not bit-identical across builds.

claims.claim: AUD-SEM-02 (audit-original): the Claude and Grok economics models have different input domains — Claude accepts zero weights and unbounded ints; Grok rejects zero weights and bounds inputs at 21e6·1e8 sats — so 'cross-model agreement' claims are scoped to the intersection domain.
claims.source_locator: audit adversarial_probes.py results; both model/allocation.py files
claims.evidence_supporting: Probes demonstrate the divergent rejections; 300 shared random vectors inside the intersection agree exactly.
claims.evidence_opposing_or_limiting: Neither package documents the domain difference; CROSS_MODEL_COMPARISON.md discusses representation (Fraction vs float) but not domain.
claims.tests_genuinely_test_or_encode: Established by the audit's own probes.
claims.weakens_or_reverses_under: Boundary inputs (zero-weight donors, >MAX_SATS values) where behavior differs by construction.
